content.claim: Hungary exhibits the highest level of socioeconomic segregation between schools in the EU (measured by concentration of advantaged vs. disadvantaged students), with a 53-point PISA performance gap between children of graduate and non-graduate parents compared to 33-point EU average.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Legal and governance lens
limitations: High segregation is documented as a systemic fact; attribution of causality specifically to the 6/8-year gimnázium entry point (versus other selection points at kindergarten entry, primary school choice, or post-16 pathways) remains an inference, not directly measured.
PISA measures outcome variance between schools but does not isolate the impact of a single structural track type.
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
